title: Angélique Porciani
text:
I enjoy working at the interplay of data, biology and real-world questions.

Contact
📧 angelique.porciani@gmail.com
GitHub
LinkedIn


Hello! I’m Angélique Porciani, a biostatistician and data analyst with over 12 years of experience working with complex data in life sciences.
Over the years, I’ve developed strong expertise in statistical modelling and data analysis with R, applied to a wide range of projects in epidemiology, health, and environmental sciences.
I particularly enjoy working with complex, time-dependent data, such as time series and longitudinal datasets, using statistical and machine learning approaches to explore patterns and support data-driven decisions. I also have experience with survival and dose-response models in the context of evaluating treatment efficacy.
More broadly, I’m interested in leveraging data to better understand complex systems and inform decision-making, whether in research or applied contexts. I’m especially motivated by projects where data can bring clarity to real-world challenges.
Beyond the technical side, I’ve coordinated projects, collaborated with diverse stakeholders, and communicated results to both technical and non-technical audiences. I enjoy working in interdisciplinary environments and value collaboration and knowledge sharing.
I’m currently seeking opportunities as a data analyst where I can apply my analytical skills to real-world data and contribute to impactful, data-driven projects.






🛠 Skills
Languages & Tools: R (tidyverse, dplyr, ggplot2), SAS, SQL, Excel, Latex, Quarto, Python, bash, Notion
Statistical Methods: descriptive statistics, regression linear or not, survival & dose-response modelling
Machine Learning: variable selection, clustering, predictive modelling
Data Visualization & Reporting: dynamics html report (Quarto), KPI creation, summary tables, clear visual communication
Soft Skills: collaboration, teaching, project coordination, interdisciplinary teamwork



Current Focus

Cleaning, transforming, and exploring complex datasets for actionable insights
Creating clear visualizations, dashboards, and indicators for decision-making
Applying statistical modelling & machine learning to real-world biological data
Time-dependent data: time series, longitudinal datasets
Survival & dose-response analyses to evaluate new molecules or interventions

title: ANIVERMATE: Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulation for malaria vector control and environmental impact
text: This project investigates the use of long-acting ivermectin formulations administered to livestock as a complementary malaria vector control strategy. It combines efficacy assessment on mosquito populations with the evaluation of environmental impact.

title: ANIVERMATE: Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulation for malaria vector control and environmental impact
text: This project investigates the use of long-acting ivermectin formulations administered to livestock as a complementary malaria vector control strategy. It combines efficacy assessment on mosquito populations with the evaluation of environmental impact.

title: ANIVERMATE: Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulation for malaria vector control and environmental impact
text:
Objectives

Characterize pharmacokinetic (PK) properties of a long-acting ivermectin formulation
Assess efficacy against Anopheles gambiae and An. coluzzii
Evaluate environmental fate and impact on non-target fauna

title: ANIVERMATE: Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulation for malaria vector control and environmental impact
text:
My role

Managed and curated experimental datasets, ensuring data quality and consistency
Performed pharmacokinetic analyses (non-compartmental modelling)
Conducted statistical modelling of efficacy outcomes (GLMM, survival analysis)
Contributed to the interpretation of results in both efficacy and ecotoxicological contexts

title: ANIVERMATE: Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulation for malaria vector control and environmental impact
text:
Methods & tools

Non-compartmental PK modelling (AUC, Tmax, half-life)
Survival analysis (Kaplan–Meier, Cox models)
Generalized linear mixed models (binomial outcomes)
R (tidyverse, ggplot2, glmmTMB, survival, coxme, PKNCA)

title: ANIVERMATE: Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulation for malaria vector control and environmental impact
text:
Key results

Sustained mosquitocidal efficacy observed for more than 6 months
Significant reduction in mosquito survival and population size through treated livestock
Effective targeting of zoophagic and opportunistic malaria vectors
Identification of measurable ecotoxicological risks for non-target organisms

title: ANIVERMATE: Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulation for malaria vector control and environmental impact
text:
Impact

Supports the development of long-lasting vector control strategies with reduced intervention frequency
Contributes to complementary approaches targeting residual malaria transmission
Informed the balance between efficacy and environmental sustainability in deployment strategies

title: ANIVERMATE: Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulation for malaria vector control and environmental impact
text:
Publications and project link
Heinrich et al. (2024)
Github link of the project : Anivermate

title: ANORYTHM: Analysis of mosquito activity rhythms and behavioural patterns
text: “Right time define a good vector”. Mosquito activity rhythms play a key role in malaria transmission. This project investigates how biological factors (e.g. insemination status) and environmental conditions (e.g. light exposure) influence the activity patterns of Anopheles coluzzii.

title: ANORYTHM: Analysis of mosquito activity rhythms and behavioural patterns
text: “Right time define a good vector”. Mosquito activity rhythms play a key role in malaria transmission. This project investigates how biological factors (e.g. insemination status) and environmental conditions (e.g. light exposure) influence the activity patterns of Anopheles coluzzii.

title: ANORYTHM: Analysis of mosquito activity rhythms and behavioural patterns
text:
Objectives

Estimate key parameters characterizing activity rhythms (period, onset time, peak activity)
Compare behavioural patterns across experimental groups
Develop a data-driven framework to model activity states and intensity over time

title: ANORYTHM: Analysis of mosquito activity rhythms and behavioural patterns
text:
My role

Managed and processed behavioural time-series datasets
Developed automated pipelines for extracting rhythm-related metrics (e.g. onset time, peak activity)
Performed statistical modelling (GLMM, zero-inflation models)
Implemented Hidden Markov Models (HMM) to classify activity states and estimate transition probabilities
Analysed and interpreted behavioural patterns across biological and environmental conditions
Supervision of master and PhD students

title: ANORYTHM: Analysis of mosquito activity rhythms and behavioural patterns
text:
Methods & tools

Time-series analysis and feature extraction
Chi-square periodogram for rhythm detection
Generalized linear mixed models (including zero-inflation)
Hidden Markov Models (HMM) for state classification
R (tidyverse, ggplot2, glmmTMB, momentuHMM, xsp)

title: ANORYTHM: Analysis of mosquito activity rhythms and behavioural patterns
text:
Key Results

Insemination and gonotrophic status significantly affect locomotor activity rhythms
Evidence of maintained activity during subjective day under constant darkness
Identification of potential daytime activity under low-light conditions
Data-driven modelling (HMM) enabled classification of activity states and quantification of behavioural dynamics over time

title: ANORYTHM: Analysis of mosquito activity rhythms and behavioural patterns
text:
Impact
Highlights the potential for residual malaria transmission during daytime Provides quantitative tools to better understand behavioural variability in malaria vectors Supports improved modelling of transmission dynamics by integrating behavioural patterns

title: ANORYTHM: Analysis of mosquito activity rhythms and behavioural patterns
text:
Publications
Traoré et al. (2021)

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text: This project aimed to evaluate new long-acting injectable formulations of ivermectin as a complementary strategy for malaria vector control. A key challenge was extending the duration of efficacy beyond current limitations (<7 days).

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text: This project aimed to evaluate new long-acting injectable formulations of ivermectin as a complementary strategy for malaria vector control. A key challenge was extending the duration of efficacy beyond current limitations (<7 days).

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Objectives

Identify the optimal formulation and dose meeting WHO Preferred Product Characteristics (HR > 4 for at least one month)
Evaluate efficacy in semi-field conditions (cattle–wild mosquitoes)
Estimate the epidemiological impact on malaria prevalence
Assess resistance development in Anopheles and Aedes
Study potential behavioural effects on mosquito host-seeking

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
My role

Designed study protocols, including sample size estimation and power calculations
Developed and implemented the statistical analysis plan
Performed data management, cleaning, and quality control
Conducted statistical analyses (survival models, GLMM, GAM, PK/PD, dose-response; epidemiological model)
Supervised students and supported partners in data analysis and interpretation
Communicated results to diverse stakeholders (scientific and non-scientific audiences)

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Methods and tools

Survival analysis, GLMM, GAM, PK/PD modelling, non-linear dose-response, epidemiological model SAP, DMP redaction, study design, sample size estimation, power calculation, indicator of efficacy creation
R (tidyverse, ggplot2, glmmTMB, coxme, mgcv, drc, PKNCA)
Data management and reporting (R, Excel, Word, Quarto)
Study design, sample size estimation, power calculation, indicator of efficacy creation

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Key Results

Sustained mosquitocidal efficacy for at least two months with HR > 4, meeting WHO PPC
Repeated treatment campaigns significantly reduced malaria prevalence (up to ~78%)
Evidence of differential resistance between Aedes and Anopheles
No significant effect on host-seeking behaviour

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Impact
Contributed to the development of a novel malaria control strategy aligned with WHO recommendations Supported the selection of a lead formulation, enabling progression to Phase I clinical trials Provided key insights for deployment strategies, including resistance management

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Publications
Sagna et al. (2023) ; Pooda et al. (2026) ; Zela et al. (2026) ; Dongmo et al. (2025)

title: Insecticide efficacy evaluation and dose optimisation
text: This project focused on evaluating the efficacy of new insecticide compounds on mosquito mortality, with the objective of supporting the development of novel vector control solutions.

title: Insecticides evaluation
text:
Context
Test of new type of insecticides on mosquitoes mortality
Objectives
- Choose of lethal dose (LD) 50 and 90 for different mosquitoes strains (different mutation conferring partial resistance to insecticides)
Methods

Dose response
Survival model : Kaplan meier curves and cox
Generalized linear models

Tools
- R (tidyverse, ggplot2, dplyr, glmmTMB, DHARMa, coxme, survival, drm, BioRssay)
Key Results
- Identification of efficient dose for 2 news type of insecticides.
Impact
Support for development of news control vector tools by a private company.

title: Mosquito behavioural analysis under insecticide exposure
text:
Environmental exposure to low concentrations of insecticides is increasingly common, particularly in mosquito breeding sites. While these concentrations may not be lethal, they can induce sub-lethal effects on behaviour and life-history traits, potentially impacting malaria transmission dynamics.
This project combines experimental design, data processing, and advanced modelling of behavioural time-series data.

title: Mosquito behavioural analysis under insecticide exposure
text:
Environmental exposure to low concentrations of insecticides is increasingly common, particularly in mosquito breeding sites. While these concentrations may not be lethal, they can induce sub-lethal effects on behaviour and life-history traits, potentially impacting malaria transmission dynamics.
This project combines experimental design, data processing, and advanced modelling of behavioural time-series data.

title: Mosquito behavioural analysis under insecticide exposure
text:
Objectives

Develop a low-cost experimental setup for video recording of mosquito larvae in field conditions
Build a pipeline for tracking larvae movement
Analyse behavioural changes in larvae and adults using statistical and modelling approaches

title: Mosquito behavioural analysis under insecticide exposure
text:
My role

Designed experimental protocols, including dose selection and sample size considerations
Developed data collection and management workflows (FAIR principles)
Processed and structured video-derived behavioural data
Implemented statistical analyses and modelling approaches (univariate, multivariate, HMM)
Developed custom functions in R to automate feature extraction (e.g. activity patterns, timing metrics)

title: Mosquito behavioural analysis under insecticide exposure
text:
Methods & tools

Time-series analysis and behavioural metrics extraction
Univariate and multivariate analysis (including PCA)
Hidden Markov Models (HMM) for activity state classification
R (tidyverse, ggplot2, glmmTMB, broom.mixed, momentuHMM, PCA, xsp)

title: Mosquito behavioural analysis under insecticide exposure
text:
Key results

Preliminary analyses indicate behavioural differences between exposed and non-exposed mosquito larvae and adults
Evidence of sub-lethal effects impacting activity patterns

title: Mosquito behavioural analysis under insecticide exposure
text:
Impact / perspective

Provides new insights into sub-lethal effects of insecticides on mosquito behaviour
Contributes to a better understanding of environmental drivers of malaria transmission
Ongoing analyses aim to refine behavioural indicators and improve modelling of exposure effects

title: RADO
text:
Ce projet est un peu à part, car il ne parle pas seulement de données, mais surtout des personnes qui travaillent avec. Il est né d’un constat simple : au centre IRD de Montpellier, beaucoup de gens manipulaient des données… sans vraiment se connaître. Et on s’est dit qu’il y avait clairement quelque chose à construire là-dessus.
Avec un collègue d’une autre unité, on a donc lancé en 2022 un groupe d’animation autour des biostatistiques : le groupe Biostat. L’idée était de créer un espace d’échange, de partage de pratiques, et tout simplement de mettre en lien les personnes.
Au fil du temps, le groupe a grandi, les échanges se sont enrichis, et on a fini par fusionner avec un autre collectif plus orienté bioinformatique. De là est né le RADO, que j’ai co-animé jusqu’à mon départ.
Animer ce réseau a été une expérience vraiment précieuse pour moi. Ça m’a permis de développer de nouvelles compétences, mais surtout de vivre de très bons moments de partage, de collaboration et de convivialité.
Et le plus chouette, c’est que le réseau continue de vivre et de s’agrandir grâce à l’énergie des personnes qui l’animent aujourd’hui.
Jetez-y un œil ici : RADO

title: Angélique Porciani
text:
I enjoy working at the interplay of data, biology and real-world questions.

Contact
📧 angelique.porciani@gmail.com
GitHub
LinkedIn


Hello! I’m Angélique Porciani, a biostatistician and data analyst with over 12 years of experience working with complex data in life sciences.
Over the years, I’ve developed strong expertise in statistical modelling and data analysis with R, applied to a wide range of projects in epidemiology, health, and environmental sciences.
I particularly enjoy working with complex, time-dependent data, such as time series and longitudinal datasets, using statistical and machine learning approaches to explore patterns and support data-driven decisions. I also have experience with survival and dose-response models in the context of evaluating treatment efficacy.
More broadly, I’m interested in leveraging data to better understand complex systems and inform decision-making, whether in research or applied contexts. I’m especially motivated by projects where data can bring clarity to real-world challenges.
Beyond the technical side, I’ve coordinated projects, collaborated with diverse stakeholders, and communicated results to both technical and non-technical audiences. I enjoy working in interdisciplinary environments and value collaboration and knowledge sharing.
I’m currently seeking opportunities as a data analyst where I can apply my analytical skills to real-world data and contribute to impactful, data-driven projects.






🛠 Skills
Languages & Tools: R (tidyverse, dplyr, ggplot2), SAS, SQL, Excel, Latex, Quarto, Python, bash, Notion
Statistical Methods: descriptive statistics, regression linear or not, survival & dose-response modelling
Machine Learning: variable selection, clustering, predictive modelling
Data Visualization & Reporting: dynamics html report (Quarto), KPI creation, summary tables, clear visual communication
Soft Skills: collaboration, teaching, project coordination, interdisciplinary teamwork



Current Focus

Cleaning, transforming, and exploring complex datasets for actionable insights
Creating clear visualizations, dashboards, and indicators for decision-making
Applying statistical modelling & machine learning to real-world biological data
Time-dependent data: time series, longitudinal datasets
Survival & dose-response analyses to evaluate new molecules or interventions

title: Insecticide efficacy evaluation and dose optimisation
text: This project focused on evaluating the efficacy of new insecticide compounds on mosquito mortality, with the objective of supporting the development of novel vector control solutions.

title: Insecticide efficacy evaluation and dose optimisation
text:
Objectives

Estimate lethal doses (LD50, LD90) across different mosquito strains
Compare efficacy across strains with varying resistance profiles

title: Insecticide efficacy evaluation and dose optimisation
text:
My role

Managed and analysed experimental datasets on mosquito mortality
Performed dose-response modelling to estimate lethal doses (LD50, LD90)
Conducted survival analyses (Kaplan–Meier, Cox models) to assess treatment effects
Applied statistical models (GLM) to compare efficacy across mosquito strains
Interpreted results to support compound evaluation and selection

title: Insecticide efficacy evaluation and dose optimisation
text:
Methods & tools

Dose-response modelling
Survival analysis (Kaplan–Meier, Cox models)
Generalized linear models (GLM)
R (tidyverse, ggplot2, glmmTMB, survival, drm, DHARMa)

title: Insecticide efficacy evaluation and dose optimisation
text:
Key Results

Identification of effective dose ranges for two new insecticide compounds
Evidence of differential efficacy across mosquito strains with resistance profiles

title: Insecticide efficacy evaluation and dose optimisation
text:
Impact

Supported decision-making in the development of new vector control tools
Contributed to the selection and optimisation of candidate insecticides for further development
Provided quantitative evidence to guide industrial R&D strategies
